pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 22 (MALICIOUS_DOMAIN="network-security-update.io"), export_result.sh line 16 (TARGET_DOMAIN="network-security-update.io"). Domain does not resolve in DNS and does not appear in web search results, confirming it is synthetic.
Code analysis: verifier.py line 29 uses metadata.get('expected_rule_value', f'*@{target_domain}') and line 65 checks if rule_value == expected_rule_value
Task description in task.json line 5 explicitly states: 'Add the note: 'Phishing campaign March 2026''. The metadata claim is incomplete, missing the date 'March 2026'.
Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 22-23 defines MALICIOUS_DOMAIN="network-security-update.io" and SENDER_EMAIL="alert@$MALICIOUS_DOMAIN"
Web search and code analysis confirm FreeScout is a legitimate open-source PHP/MySQL helpdesk application (https://freescout.net/, https://github.com/freescout-help-desk/freescout)
Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 24 defines SUBJECT="CRITICAL: Server Access Expiring"

privileged_info_summary: This task uses a synthetically created phishing email scenario within FreeScout, an open-source helpdesk application. The phishing email originates from alert@network-security-update.io (a non-existent synthetic domain) with subject 'CRITICAL: Server Access Expiring'. The task requires agents to create a global blacklist rule with the format '*@network-security-update.io' and add the note 'Phishing campaign March 2026'. All core metadata values (target_domain, expected_rule_value, sender_email) are consistent with the task code, though the expected_note_content metadata is incomplete, omitting the date component.
